title: Lesson 9: (Kana) - Lesson 9: (Hiragana) "だ, ぢ, づ, で, ど"

intro_cards.title: ぢ and づ Are Rare
intro_cards.body:
While だ, で, and ど appear in many words, ぢ and づ are rarely used in modern Japanese.

In almost all cases, じ (ji) is used instead of ぢ, and ず (zu) is used instead of づ. They sound identical.

You may occasionally see づ in compound words like つづく (to continue), where the base word starts with つ. But in general, you can expect to see じ and ず far more often.
